Guideline: Use Astro pages and layouts intentionally
Description: Keep route files lean and share chrome in layouts
Do: Create reusable layouts and page-specific Astro files
Don't: Duplicate shell markup in every page

Description: Keep most of the page static and hydrate only what needs JS
Do: Use client directives on isolated interactive components
Don't: Ship whole pages as hydrated SPA by default

Description: Hydrate at the moment that matches the UX need
Do: client:visible or client:idle for deferred widgets

Guideline: Fetch on the server by default
Description: Astro pages can load data at build or request time
Do: Use frontmatter fetch or server endpoints
Don't: useEffect style client fetching for first render

Guideline: Use content collections for structured content
Description: Schema-backed collections improve safety and tooling
Do: Define collection schemas and query with getCollection

Description: Optimize images and avoid layout shift
Do: Use Image or Picture from astro:assets

Description: Keep route naming obvious and colocate route assets
Do: Use dynamic routes and content-driven slugs

Description: Use scoped component styles and one clear global entrypoint
Do: Scope styles to components and central tokens

Description: Astro wins when most UI is static HTML

Do: Choose one client framework per area
Code Good: astro add react for one widget
Code Bad: Three UI runtimes on one page

Guideline: Use API routes or actions intentionally
Description: Choose the simplest server path for form handling
Do: Use Astro endpoints or server actions style patterns
Don't: Post to ad-hoc client-only services by default

Guideline: Use view transitions when they add clarity
Description: Astro supports smooth navigation without overdoing motion
Do: Use view transitions for app-like navigation

Guideline: Use metadata and semantic content well
Description: Astro sites often care about SEO and crawlability
Do: Set title description canonical and OG tags
Code Bad: One global title for every page

Description: Static and server output solve different problems
Do: Choose static for content-heavy sites and server for request-time needs
Don't: Default blindly without matching the product

Guideline: Use the CLI and integrations to stay aligned
Description: Astro tooling captures common setup safely
Do: Use astro add and official integrations
Code Bad: npm package soup with custom wiring
